_doc: Real responses captured from the original session's .local/investigations/raw/. First-match-wins: list specific patterns before generic ones. `expected_calls` declares the minimum-coverage patterns the test expects the agent to exercise (compared against .calls.jsonl by tmp/coverage_report.py after the run).

rules._doc: Catch-all: every `docsai ask` (regardless of topic) returns the same Orchestrator-focused retry guidance, mirroring the single docsai call observed in the original session. If the agent asks docsai about Integration Service it receives non-blocking but off-topic content — relying on docsai for IS guidance is not part of the success path.

expected_calls.description: Agent looks up the runner's personal folder via the documented Personal-friendly endpoint.
Agent fetches the pinned faulted job's details to read the error message and failing activity.
Agent lists OneDrive connections in the runner's workspace to compare against the workflow's connection ID.

OPTIONAL — Agent may ping the failing connection ID to confirm the runtime error verbatim, but a more efficient path confirms cross-workspace ownership directly from the project's connection resource file + Main.xaml. Both paths are valid; the source-file path is optimal. min:0 means tracked-but-not-required.
